page: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, running at 2.5 GHz with the AWS Neoverse-N1 model. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average performance across most multi-threaded and memory-bound workloads. The server achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48574.31 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11649.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1894.03 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 163.55 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, single-core and single-threaded performance represents a notable tradeoff, with weak ratings in single-threaded gzip compression (20.33 GB/sec), single-threaded bzip3 decompression (7.57 GB/sec), and Geekbench text processing (1213.0).

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based Graviton architecture delivers a high density of dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, offering a favorable performance-per-resource profile for multi-threaded tasks. This server is optimized for workloads such as multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations. It is also capable of handling moderate LLM inference tasks, demonstrating average prompt processing speeds of 32.02 tokens/sec on 7B models.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server utilizing the arm64 architecture with 32 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors running at 2.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data shows average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48574.31 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11649.0. However, single-core and single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression and text processing, exhibit weaker performance. This server is designed for multi-threaded workloads, including software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and moderate language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated ARM64 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 12 Gbps network baseline, delivering balanced multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6g.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated cores, 64 GB RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring thirty-two dedicated cores and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 ARM64 physical cores
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Delivers 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Demonstrates average multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark scores
Tradeoffs include weaker single-core performance and lack of local storage
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing
